title: Non-capturing groups without quantifier should not be used
description: Non-capturing groups in regular expressions (?:...) are useful when you want to group expressions without capturing them for back-references. However, if a non-capturing group is not followed by a quantifier, it is redundant and can be removed to simplify the regex.
recommendation: Remove non-capturing groups that are not followed by a quantifier to simplify the regular expression.
impact: Redundant non-capturing groups make regular expressions harder to read and maintain, without providing any functional benefit.

bad: Pattern pattern = Pattern.compile("(?:abc)def"); // Non-capturing group without quantifier
good: Pattern pattern = Pattern.compile("abcdef"); // Simplified regex, group removed

checks.message: Remove non-capturing groups that are not followed by a quantifier to simplify the regular expression.
